name: Uncomplicated UTI
context: Otherwise healthy outpatient, no recent abx, no urologic abnormalities. Female non-pregnant.

firstLine.rationale: Local E. coli susceptibility ~97%. Excellent urinary concentration.
Local E. coli susceptibility ~75%. Avoid if local resistance > 20% or recent TMP-SMX use.

alternatives.rationale: Cephalexin reasonable second-line, especially for ESBL concerns are low.

avoid.reason: Reserve fluoroquinolones — local E. coli FQ susceptibility ~70–75%; ADRs (tendon, QT, neurologic).
Local E. coli susceptibility ~52% — inadequate empiric coverage.

notes: Confirm uncomplicated criteria. For pregnant, complicated, or pyelo cases, see Complicated UTI / Pyelonephritis.

name: Complicated UTI / Pyelonephritis
context: Pyelonephritis, urosepsis, indwelling catheter, male, pregnant, or urologic abnormalities.

firstLine.rationale: Local gram-negative susceptibility ~81–92%. First-line empiric for inpatient pyelo.

alternatives.rationale: If sepsis, recent broad-spectrum abx, or healthcare exposure. Avoid for HECK-Yes organisms.
If Pseudomonas risk or AmpC concern.

avoid.reason: Local E. coli susceptibility ~63–65% — borderline coverage for empiric.
Does NOT achieve adequate tissue levels — urinary tract concentration only. Don't use for pyelo.

duration: 5–7 days (depending on agent — see Shorter Is Better)
notes: If patient has had recent abx, ESBL history, or healthcare exposure — consider cefepime or carbapenem and consult ID.

name: Community-Acquired Pneumonia (CAP)
context: Outpatient or inpatient (non-ICU) without risk factors for MRSA or Pseudomonas.

firstLine.dose: 1–2 g IV q24h + macrolide or doxycycline
firstLine.rationale: Inpatient non-ICU. Covers S. pneumoniae, H. influenzae, Moraxella.

alternatives.rationale: Respiratory fluoroquinolone monotherapy. Use when beta-lactam contraindicated.

avoid.reason: Not needed empirically unless MRSA risk factors (recent hospitalization, prior MRSA, severe CAP).

notes: Per IDSA 2019. Add vancomycin OR linezolid for MRSA risk; add piperacillin-tazobactam OR cefepime for Pseudomonas risk.

name: Hospital / Ventilator-Associated Pneumonia
context: Pneumonia developing ≥ 48 h after admission or ≥ 48 h after intubation.

firstLine.dose: 2 g IV q8h + vancomycin 15–20 mg/kg IV q8–12h
firstLine.rationale: Empiric coverage of Pseudomonas + MRSA. Local Pseudomonas cefepime susceptibility ~93–94%.

alternatives.rationale: If no AmpC concern. ICU Pseudomonas pip-tazo ~92%.
If ESBL or carbapenem-needed pathogens suspected.

avoid.reason: No Pseudomonas coverage — inadequate empiric for HAP/VAP.

notes: Per IDSA 2016. De-escalate based on culture data. Add linezolid alternative if vancomycin contraindicated.

name: Skin & Soft Tissue Infection
context: Non-purulent cellulitis vs purulent (abscess/furuncle). Severity determines coverage.

firstLine.dose: Cephalexin 500 mg PO QID (mild) or cefazolin 1–2 g IV q8h (mod)
firstLine.rationale: Non-purulent cellulitis: targets Streptococcus. Local MSSA cefazolin ~100% (via oxacillin).
Purulent / suspected MRSA. Local MRSA TMP-SMX ~65–73%.

alternatives.dose: 300–450 mg PO TID or 600 mg IV q8h
alternatives.rationale: Beta-lactam allergy. Local MRSA clindamycin ~69–91% — variable, check sensitivities.
PO option for MRSA outpatient bridge.

avoid.reason: Poor Strep coverage and overuse driving resistance.

notes: Per IDSA 2014. Source control (I&D) is the primary treatment for abscess.

name: Intra-Abdominal Infection
context: Community-acquired complicated IAI (perforated appendix, diverticulitis, secondary peritonitis).

firstLine.dose: 1–2 g IV q24h + metronidazole 500 mg IV q8h
firstLine.rationale: Covers gram-negatives + anaerobes. Standard for community-acquired moderate severity.

alternatives.rationale: Single-agent for severe/healthcare-associated. Covers Pseudomonas + anaerobes.
If pip-tazo unavailable. Note Enterococcus coverage added by ampicillin.

avoid.reason: Insufficient gram-negative coverage as monotherapy locally.

notes: Source control (drainage, surgery) is essential. Per IDSA/SIS. Add Enterococcus coverage (ampicillin) for severe / immunocompromised.
